content:
Run the following command.
nc mercury.picoctf.net 35652 returns an output of numbers in decimal format. We have to create a python script in order to convert from this decimal output to ASCII text.
First save the output to a file named flag.txt
nc mercury.picoctf.net 35652 > flag.txt After that, we need to place the numbers in the same line using awk.
cat flag.txt | awk '{print}' ORS='' After we got the output in the same line, here is the python script that converts decimal to ASCII text.
def decimal_to_ascii(decimal_str): string_convert = "" for decimal_char in decimal_str.split(" "): string_convert += chr(int(decimal_char, 10)) return string_convert val = input("Enter decimal format: ") print(decimal_to_ascii(val))
summary:
Run the following command.
nc mercury.picoctf.net 35652 returns an output of numbers in decimal format. We have to create a python script in order to convert from this decimal output to ASCII text.
First save the output to a file named flag.txt
nc mercury.picoctf.net 35652 > flag.txt After that, we need to place the numbers in the same line using awk.
cat flag.txt | awk '{print}' ORS='' After we got the output in the same line, here is the python script that converts decimal to ASCII text.
title: Nice Netcat

title: Obedient Cat
Python Wrangling

content:
Download the warm program.
cat warm; returns Hello user! Pass me a -h to learn what I can do!-hOh, help? I actually don’t do much, but I do have this flag here:
We can already see the flag here, but we can take it a step further. Give permissions of the executable with:
chmod +x warm ./warm -h returns the flag.

content:
Hi, I’m Marios. I’m from Greece 🇬🇷.
Currently working as a Software Developer and MSc Cyber Security student @ Leeds Beckett University 🇬🇧.
This is a personal blog, in which I will write stuff about Cyber Security, with topics such as Penetration Testing, Bug Bounty, Cryptography, CTF and more.
You can contact me via email here.
